data.title: Optus restores nbn services across Brisbane, parts of Queensland, website shows
data.description:
Australian telecom company Optus has fully restored its National Broadband Network (nbn) services in Brisbane and other affected areas of Queensland following a recent outage, according to an update on its website on Thursday.

Optus, owned by Singapore Telecommunications (SingTel) , did not immediately respond to a Reuters' request for comment, while SingTel is also yet to respond.

The outage on Wednesday left almost 100,000 Optus customers across Brisbane and southeast Queensland unable to dial "000", Australia's emergency number, after an nbn disruption, according to media reports.

The outage also prevented some landline users from reaching emergency services, the reports added.

Media outlets also reported that a spokesperson of the company told Brisbane radio the disruption had been caused by a network server failure.

data.title: FDA approves Axogen's nerve repair graft
data.description:
The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has approved Axogen's nerve repair graft, the health regulator said on Wednesday, paving the way for 12 years of potential market exclusivity in the United States.

Axogen's Avance Nerve Graft, a human tissue-based product, is designed to repair damaged peripheral nerves without requiring a second surgery to harvest nerve tissue from the patient, thereby reducing complications and supporting faster recovery.

Unlike traditional treatments that require surgeons to harvest healthy nerve tissue from another part of the patient's body, Avance uses donor nerve tissue that has been carefully processed to remove cells while preserving the natural structure essential for nerve regeneration.

The treatment was previously classified as donated human tissue and but with the FDA approval will now be considered for the treatment of sensory nerve discontinuities in adults and pediatric patients aged one month and older.

The approval could give Avance 12 years of market exclusivity in the U.S. and enable expanded insurance coverage as more doctors begin using it as a standard treatment for peripheral nerve repair, analysts have said.

"Avance can bridge gaps in damaged nerves and support nerve function restoration," said Vinay Prasad, chief medical and scientific officer and director, FDA's Center for Biologics Evaluation and Research.

Current alternatives to Avance Nerve Graft include autografts, where surgeons use a patient's own nerve from another body part to treat damaged peripheral nerves, which can cause additional pain and complications.

Peripheral nerve injuries are damage to the nerves outside the brain and spinal cord. The nerves carry signals between the brain, spinal cord, and the rest of the body, controlling movement, sensation, and bodily functions.

Injury to peripheral nerves can cause symptoms such as pain, numbness, weakness, and loss of function in the affected areas.

data.title: Key facts: Tether mints $1B on Tron; stable reserves generate $500M; gold holdings reach 116 tons
data.description:
• Tether (USDTUSD) minted $1 billion on the Tron network, boosting stablecoin inflows and aiding the crypto market rebound. Its market cap rose by $1.3 billion to $184.6 billion in November.12
  • K33's analysis shows Tether's reserves are stable, generating $500 million monthly from U.S. Treasury yields, with $7 billion in excess equity over $184.5 billion in liabilities.3
  • In Q3 2025, Tether bought 26 tons of gold, becoming the largest buyer that quarter and raising its total to about 116 tons, ranking it among the top 30 gold holders.4

data.title: Key facts: Solana Surges; $60M in Short Liquidations; CME Reports Record Crypto Contracts
data.description:
• In the past 24 hours, Solana (SOLUSD) saw nearly $60 million in short liquidations as its price rose above $140, with $39 million on Solana contracts and $19 million on exchanges.1
  • In November 2025, CME Group reported a record 424,000 daily contracts in crypto derivatives, totaling $13.2 billion, a 78% increase, driven by new Solana futures.2
  • Solana's DeFi ecosystem is recovering, driven by rising memecoin trading volume and renewed confidence in its speed, reinforcing its role as a major player in the altcoin market.3

data.title: Key facts: Fusaka Upgrade Enhances Ethereum; Price Drops Below $2,800
data.description:
• The Fusaka upgrade for Ethereum is live, enhancing data capacity, reducing transaction costs, and improving usability. It introduces PeerDAS, significantly scaling Ethereum and layer 2 solutions.123
  • Ethereum's price has dropped below key moving averages, indicating a bearish trend. It is now trading at approximately $2,800, testing previous support after falling from the $3,600–$3,800 range.4
  • Fusaka has improved Ethereum's security by adjusting blob fees to network congestion, capping transaction gas usage, and adding passkey-style authentication to boost institutional adoption.5

data.title: Key facts: Cardano up 14.53% to $0.4518; Hoskinson shares vision; Solana co-founder praises resilience
data.description:
• Cardano (ADA) surged 14.53% in 24 hours to $0.4518, hitting a weekly high and outperforming the market's average rise of 6.8% during the crypto recovery.1
  • Charles Hoskinson, co-founder of Cardano, has outlined an ambitious vision for the network as it prepares for developments leading up to 2026.2
  • Cardano's recent network recovery has received recognition from various ecosystems, with Solana co-founder Anatoly Yakovenko publicly praising the protocol's resilience.3

data.title: Key facts: Bitcoin nears breakout; $6.9T processed in payments; MicroStrategy faces MSCI removal
data.description:
• Bitcoin is nearing a breakout above a bearish trendline, with a recent higher low established around $83K, while currently testing significant horizontal resistance at approximately $93K.1
  • Bitcoin processed $6.9 trillion in payments in 90 days, rivaling Visa and Mastercard. Institutional investors now hold about 6.7 million BTC in ETFs and corporate treasuries.2
  • MicroStrategy, the top corporate Bitcoin holder, may be removed from MSCI indices due to a rule classifying firms with over 50% in digital assets as 'digital asset funds.'34

data.title: Key facts: Ripple Labs settles SEC dispute; CME Group reports record crypto trading; Firelight Finance launches XRP staking protocol
data.description:
• In May 2025, Ripple Labs settled with the SEC for $50 million, resolving a protracted legal dispute that had previously impeded the growth of XRP.1
  • In November 2025, CME Group saw record trading in crypto derivatives, averaging 424,000 contracts daily worth $13.2 billion, a 78% rise, boosted by XRP futures.2
  • Firelight Finance has launched an XRP staking protocol on Flare, introducing stXRP for 1:1 bridging. While rewards are pending, it aims to enhance DeFi use cases and attract institutional interest.345

data.title: Key facts: GLNK ETF Launches with $13.8M Volume; Chainlink Rallies 20%
data.description:
• The GLNK ETF, converting Grayscale's Chainlink trust to a public product, launched with no fees, trading 1.17 million shares and achieving $13.8 million in volume on its first day.1
  • On December 2, 2025, Chainlink (LINK) saw a notable rally, with the token increasing by approximately 18-20% to hit an intraday high of $14.57, outperforming other major cryptocurrencies such as XRP.2

data.title: Key facts: 21Shares files Dogecoin ETF application; futures surge 10,187%; price rebounds over 13%
data.description:
• 21Shares has filed an updated application for a Dogecoin ETF with the U.S. SEC, proposing a 0.50% management fee and plans for listing on Nasdaq under the ticker TDOG.1
  • Dogecoin's futures on Bitmex surged 10,187%, with a volume of $120.76 million in 24 hours, indicating increased trader interest in its recent price changes.2
  • Dogecoin rebounded from a key support at $0.138 after dropping to $0.13443, rising to $0.152 for a gain of over 13%, supported by the 200-week moving average.3

data.title: Microsoft's stock falls on reported AI sales woes. Should investors worry?
data.description:
By Britney Nguyen

Adjusting sales quotas early in the fiscal year is not surprising, says one analyst who sees weakness in the stock as a buying opportunity

Microsoft shares fell Wednesday after a report said the company had lowered some sales and growth targets.

The latest source of Wall Street debate is a report saying that Microsoft lowered sales-growth targets for some artificial-intelligence software.

To investors who have been increasingly concerned about a bubble, the report came as validation that AI isn't so easy to monetize. But some analysts on Wall Street said investors shouldn't be worried.

At the center of the debate is a report from The Information, which said Microsoft (MSFT) had lowered quotas across a number of divisions after salespeople missed targets for certain AI products in the fiscal year that ended in June. The story cited people in Microsoft's Azure cloud unit.

Evercore ISI analyst Kirk Materne wrote in a Wednesday note that it is still early in the current fiscal year, and that "the key with quota setting is not to make it too hard or too easy to clear the bar."

Therefore, Materne said he doesn't "view quota adjustments, especially at the product level, as a huge surprise" at this point in the year.

Mizuho desk-based analyst Jordan Klein thinks Microsoft's move wouldn't necessarily have been a sudden decision, meaning the company's guidance for Azure growth likely already factored in the lowered quotas when the company reported earnings results in October.

See more: Why Microsoft's stock fell in the face of upbeat earnings

"We do not even know how high the prior sales quotas were" between last year and this year, Klein said. "Maybe they were artificially too high and are much more realistic and still pretty good."

A Microsoft spokesperson said in a statement shared with MarketWatch that the article "inaccurately combines the concepts of growth and sales quotas, which shows their lack of understanding of the way a sales organization works and is compensated."

The spokesperson said that "aggregate sales quotas for AI products have not been lowered."

The company's Foundry platform, which allows users to develop and deploy AI agents and applications, is reportedly one of the offerings for which sales quotas were lowered. Cutting targets is uncommon for the tech giant and could reflect a resistance to new products, The Information said.

However, Foundry only makes up "a tiny part of Azure," Materne said. While cuts aren't a great look for the company, they "might speak more to the fact that both Snowflake and Databricks are formidable competitors/partners in this area and there could be a view that it's better to partner [versus] compete at certain levels of the stack," he said.

Recapping a recent meeting with the company, Materne said Microsoft "could not be more direct that demand is strong, especially for Azure," which is seeing demand overtake capacity.

Microsoft's stock fell as much as 3% on Wednesday morning and ended the day off 2.5%.

Taken together with recent market jitters over OpenAI's dominance after the release of Google's (GOOG) (GOOGL) Gemini 3, Materne said he isn't surprised that the report sent Microsoft's stock down.

Don't miss: As ChatGPT turns 3, here's what's crashing the party

"We would be using the weakness as a buying opportunity into 2026," Materne said.

Klein, meanwhile, said the report "is backward looking to June [versus] forward looking."

-Britney Nguyen

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: BetMakers's CrownBet Agreement Validates Tech Platform — Market Talk
data.description: BetMakers Technology's exclusive five-year agreement with Betfair Australia to launch wagering brand CrownBet excites its bull at Ord Minnett. BetMakers will be the sole technology provider for the launch of CrownBet, expected to happen in 1Q of 2026. The agreement establishes a partnership with Betfair, whose parent is casino owner Crown Resorts. "This positive announcement from BetMakers illustrates the continued momentum of the business," analyst Milo Ferris says. "The signing of a Tier-1 customer like Betfair validates BetMakers's core technology platform, and we expect recent success to continue and drive the business forward toward medium-term targets." BetMakers is up 5.7% at A$0.185, still below Ord Minnett's A$0.26/share target price. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)
